description:
Goal: split shared native codegen at the real seam. Signal: backend emission/finalization mechanics are reusable without dragging `ModulePlan`, `PlannedProgram`, or `AbiFacts` through the Compiler2 path.

Change:
- extract the reusable native backend compilation core from the planner-shaped wrapper in `ir_codegen`
- make the remaining planner wrapper an outer compatibility shell, not the center of the design
- remove planner-only threading from the shared core where the backend does not truly need it

Why:
- as long as the shared codegen core is planner-shaped, Compiler2 can only reach it by rebuilding the old world on the side

Checks:
- the extracted native codegen core has a smaller, fact-driven input surface
- Compiler2-native tests can enter the shared native backend without calling old preparation helpers
title: Compiler2 native backends: split shared codegen at the planner seam
